content.verdict: Nay Palad Hideaway is the most charming and soulful of Siargao's luxury retreats — 10 traditional nipa hut casitas arranged around a still lagoon in the coconut groves of Pacifico village, where the eco-conscious kitchen forages from the surrounding land and sea, the yoga pavilion opens to mangrove forest, and the sense of genuine hideaway is total. The combination of Siargao's surf and island beauty with this meditative, low-impact retreat makes it one of the most moving small hotels in the Philippines.
content.best_room: Lagoon-facing casitas offer the most serene setting — the still water of the lagoon, the mangrove reflections, and the sunrise visible through the nipa palm roof structure create a genuinely meditative morning. All nipa huts are built using traditional materials and techniques, with each one slightly different in character. Request a lagoon-front position with the largest private deck available.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Lagoon Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: The resort is in the northern Pacifico area of Siargao — more remote and quieter than the General Luna surf zone, accessible by a 40-minute drive from the airport. The coconut grove path to the lagoon, the nipa huts reflected in still water, and the sound of nothing but birds and the distant Pacific immediately establish why guests return annually. Dinner is the kitchen's forager's menu: whatever was caught and gathered that day, prepared with skill.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Pacifico Surf Break
itinerary_7_nights.description: Nay Palad is located near the Pacifico surf break — a consistent left-hander that is gentler and less crowded than Cloud 9, making it excellent for intermediate surfers or those learning. The resort arranges surfboards and guides for guests who want to surf. Non-surfers can swim in the lagoon's calm waters or walk the deserted black-sand beach nearby. Afternoon: group yoga on the open pavilion.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Forager's Cooking Experience
itinerary_7_nights.description: The kitchen is one of Nay Palad's defining features — the resident chef uses an entirely local sourcing philosophy, collaborating with Siargao fishermen, community farmers, and the resort's own kitchen garden. Join a morning foraging walk to understand what is growing and available: coconut heart, wild spinach, and seaweed from the lagoon edge. Cook the harvest together in the afternoon and eat dinner at the communal table.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sugba Lagoon & Del Carmen Mangroves
itinerary_7_nights.description: The resort boat takes you south through Siargao's eastern coast to Sugba Lagoon — the vast, clear-green interior lagoon that is the island's most spectacular non-surf natural attraction. Kayak the inner channels, swim from the floating raft, and explore the surrounding mangrove tributaries by outrigger. Return for a sunset reflection ceremony on the Nay Palad lagoon deck with locally brewed coconut wine.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Full Wellness Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Nay Palad's approach to wellness is rooted in local Siargao traditions rather than imported spa formats. The in-house healer offers traditional Filipino Hilot massage, herbal steam baths made from lagoon plants, and sound healing sessions using shells and local bamboo instruments. Book a full day of treatments beginning at dawn, punctuated by meals and lagoon swimming, for the most profound rest of the trip.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cloud 9 & General Luna Day Trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Take the resort vehicle south to General Luna for a day combining the famous Cloud 9 surf boardwalk spectacle with lunch on Alona Beach equivalent (Siargao's GL beach strip) and an afternoon at the town's best cafe-restaurants. The contrast between Nay Palad's absolute solitude and GL's beach-town energy gives perspective to both. Return north in time for the nipa hut lanterns coming on at dusk.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: Your last morning at Nay Palad: dawn on the lagoon deck as the sun rises over the coconut grove and the still water mirrors the sky. Breakfast is the kitchen's best: fresh coconut, wild-caught fish, and island-roasted cacao drink. The 40-minute drive back to the airport passes through the interior coconut landscape of Siargao — a reminder that this island remains, despite its growing fame, genuinely itself.

caveats: Nay Palad Hideaway is in Pacifico, the remote north of Siargao — 40 minutes from the airport and General Luna's restaurants and nightlife. For couples who want easy access to Siargao's social scene, the isolation can feel limiting. For those seeking true retreat, it is the point.
The resort was significantly affected by Typhoon Odette in December 2021 — it has since been rebuilt, but confirm current operational status and whether all casitas are operational before booking.

At 10 casitas, the resort can feel very social if most are occupied — the communal dining format and shared common spaces mean you interact with other guests more than at conventional luxury hotels. If total privacy is paramount, consider DEDON Island where the pool-side configuration is more separated.

faqs.question: Is Nay Palad Hideaway good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Very much so for couples who value authenticity, soul, and the meditative quality of a genuinely remote lagoon setting. The nipa hut architecture, the forager's kitchen, the Pacifico surf access, and Nay Palad's overall philosophy of lightness and local rootedness create one of Asia's most emotionally resonant small hotels. Couples wanting ultra-luxury service and finishes will prefer DEDON Island.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: March to May for the calmest and sunniest conditions on Siargao. September to November for surf season at Pacifico and Cloud 9. December to February is good but can have occasional rough weather. The typhoon season risk is highest September to November in active years — check forecasts. Avoid peak typhoon months in particularly active seasons.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Ten casitas means Nay Palad fills quickly in peak season — book at least 3 months ahead. The full-day wellness programme should be specified at booking so the healer can prepare. Sugba Lagoon boat trips should be arranged through the resort on arrival. The kitchen operates a set daily menu — dietary requirements must be communicated in advance.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: Not officially, but the remote location, lagoon setting, and communal dining philosophy make it almost exclusively an adult guest property. Families are very rare — the resort appeals primarily to couples, solo travellers seeking restoration, and small groups of friends. Children are technically welcome but the experience is not designed for them.

faqs.question: What's the best room for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: Lagoon-front casitas with the largest deck — these offer the most serene setting, with the still water reflecting the nipa palms and the sunrise. All casitas are traditional in construction and beautiful in character; the lagoon position is the primary differentiator. Specify lagoon-facing and most private position at booking.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly from Manila or Cebu to Siargao's Sayak Airport (IAO). From the airport, the resort vehicle collects you for the 40-minute drive north to Pacifico. The hotel arranges all transfers — coordinate arrival and departure times with the resort team. General Luna is 40 minutes south and Cloud 9 is accessible from there by tricycle.
